title: Saato
content:

{{Quote|Slippery little thing, aren't you, Jedi?|Saato to Anakin Skywalker, during their final fight|<ref name="NDA" />}}
'''Saato''' was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Witch/Legends|witch]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] who left the [[Witches of Dathomir]] to work as a [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolyte]] under [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. In [[21 BBY/Legends|21.17 BBY]], during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], she was sent to the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] alongside the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] to capture the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Raala Ponchar]], who had knowledge on a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Rise of the Cortosis Battle Droids|operation]] to create [[C-B3 cortosis battle droid/Legends|droids]] invulnerable to [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] attacks. She encountered [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], who she [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]], though he later escaped and [[Death/Legends|killed]] Saato near the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]].

==Biography==
===Search for Raala Ponchar===
{{Quote|Inform Mistress Saato that the farm girl was not with this group. We'll rendezvous with the others at the farms north of Mos Espa and continue the search.|A battle droid to another, on their assignment from Saato to locate Ponchar|<ref name="NDA" />}}
[[File:CB3CortosisBattleDroid-NEGTD.png|thumb|left|250px|Raala Ponchar, whom Saato was sent to capture, had obtained knowledge on the C-B3 cortosis battle droid (pictured).]]
[[Homeworld/Legends|Hailing]] from<ref name="nec" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]], the [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Saato was a member of the [[Witches of Dathomir]],<ref name="nec" /> a culture of [[Witch/Legends|witch]] [[Coven/Legends|covens]] who practiced [[The Force/Legends|Force-based]] [[Dathomir Magic|magic]].<ref name="COPL">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref> In [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], she left Dathomir and joined the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] and [[Head of State/Legends|leader]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of the Independent Systems]] [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], also known under the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] moniker [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Tyranus, as one of his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentices]]<ref name="nec" /> known as [[Dark Acolyte/Legends|Dark Acolytes]].<ref name="FF" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the Confederacy of Independent Systems revealed itself to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], and launched the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] against the both groups.<ref name="NDA" /> In [[21 BBY/Legends|21.17 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the Separatists [[Rise of the Cortosis Battle Droids|began]] the manufacturing of the [[C-B3 cortosis battle droid/Legends|C-B3 cortosis battle droid]], a new type of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] made of the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-resistant material [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]], to better combat [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] on battlefields.<ref name="nec" /> Living on<ref name="NDA" /> the Outer Rim<ref name="TEA" /> planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Republic [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Raala Ponchar]] discovered the operation and notified the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]].<ref name="NDA" />

In response, Saato was dispatched to Tatooine alongside the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] and a portion of the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]], with the mission to find and [[Prisoner/Legends|imprison]] Ponchar. Meanwhile, the Jedi Order had dispatched Jedi Knight [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to [[Mission to Tatooine (Anakin Skywalker)|rescue]] the spy. Saato sent out a group of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] to aid her in her search; however, they encountered Skywalker, who destroyed them. The Sith witch eventually discovered Ponchar working at the [[Bellek's homestead|homestead]] of the [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]] [[Bellek]], north of the [[City/Legends|city]] [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]]. Saato took Ponchar and ordered her [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to destroy Bellek's [[Farm/Legends|farmstead]], as well as the surrounding homesteads. Saato imprisoned the spy in the [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]] [[Prison/Legends|dungeons]] of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], who was allied with the Separatists through his [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|criminal empire]].<ref name="NDA" />

===Fight with Skywalker and Death===
{{Quote|Count Dooku was right: your Master has prepared you well. But you won't free yourself so easily from the pit of the great Sarlacc!|Saato to Skywalker, directly before their battle|<ref name="NDA" />}}
Skywalker arrived to a homestead near Bellek's shortly afterward, and talked to one of the people outside, who informed him of the attack. He then set out to find Bellek, who had been captured by the native [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. Upon saving the [[Farmer/Legends|farmer]], Bellek directed him to Jabba's Palace to find Saato; however, when he arrived, Sing greeted him and threatened to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him. Before the two could fight, Saato arrived and [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetically]] gripped and strangled Skywalker to subdue him, before imprisoning him in the palace dungeons. Saato informed Dooku that the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] would no longer be a threat to their operation.<ref name="NDA" />

[[File:Saato_vs_Anakin.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Saato and Anakin Skywalker duel on Tatooine.]]
Waking up in the palace's dungeons, Skywalker met and spoke with Ponchar about her intel. The two escaped and parted ways, before Skywalker stole a [[Bantha-II cargo skiff/Legends|desert skiff]] to escape. However, Saato tailed Skywalker in her own [[Skiff/Legends|skiff]] and shot at him using its [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]]. After [[Laser/Legends|laserfire]] hit Skywalker's skiff, it crashed into the sand below and Saato situated her skiff next to the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]], with intent to force the Jedi into the pit. As Skywalker approached, Saato ignited her two [[Shoto lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber shotos]] and [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueled]] the Jedi, complementing him on his ability as described by Dooku. However, the Jedi was able to defeat and kill the witch.<ref name="NDA" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Anakin Skywalker, dispatched to investigate Jabba's operations, killed Dooku's Sith witch Saato.|Voren Na'al in his chronicle, on Saato's death by Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="nec" />}}
In the [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|second edition]] of his chronicle of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history known as ''[[The Essential Chronology]]'', published to the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] in [[36 ABY]], [[Author/Legends|author]] and [[archivist emeritus]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] [[Historical Council]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] included many events of the Clone Wars which had not been present in his first edition. This included the beginning of Saato's partnership with Dooku and her death at the hands of Skywalker.<ref name="nec" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Others included Sev'rance Tann, a Chiss who had been brought into civilized space with her lover Vandalor by Darth Sidious, and Saato, a Witch from Dathomir with a fearsome tattooed face.|Voren Na'al, in the second edition of his chronicle|<ref name="nec" />}}
Saato had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and pale skin. She had black [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]],<ref name="NDA" /> which Na'al described in his chronicle as "fearsome."<ref name="nec" /> In her [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|battle]] against Skywalker, she taunted him several [[Time/Legends|times]], but was able to note the Jedi's capability.<ref name="NDA" /> 

==Powers and abilities==
Like all Witches of Dathomir,<ref name="COPL" /> Saato was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and exhibited several [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], such as the ability to use telekinesis to grip objects. She was also versed in lightsaber dueling and was a practitioner of [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]],<ref name="NDA" /> a style of [[Sword/Legends|sword]] combat characterized by the use of two [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]].<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref> She often struck with her shotos in a whirlwinding manner, with focus on agility and acrobatics.<ref name="NDA" />

==Equipment==
Saato wielded two red-bladed lightsaber shotos and wore a red [[Clothing/Legends|outfit]].<ref name="NDA" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:NewDroidArmyBox.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Saato first appeared in ''Star Wars: The New Droid Army''.]]
Saato first appeared in the [[2002]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The New Droid Army]]'', published by [[THQ]]. In the game, Anakin Skywalker, the player character, can kill Saato in two ways: by killing her through the use of his [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] or by pushing her into the Great Pit of Carkoon.<ref name="NDA" />

The article "[[Everything You Always Wanted to Know about the Nightsisters but Were Afraid to Ask]]," published in the [[Star Wars Insider 122|one hundred twenty-second issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|122|Everything You Always Wanted to Know about the Nightsisters but Were Afraid to Ask}}</ref> on [[December 14]], [[2010]],<ref name="Release date">{{SWArchive|url=fans/media_news/star_wars_insider_122/index.html|text=Exclusive Howard Chaykin Cover and Poster in ''Star Wars Insider'' #122}}</ref> states that the Witches of Dathomir were made up of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirians]]. It also establishes that Dathomirians are pale-skinned,<ref name="Insider" /> and author [[Dave Wolverton]]'s [[1994]] novel ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'' establishes the Human Witches of Dathomir with the same skin tone.<ref name="COPL" /> As no source confirms Saato's [[Species/Legends|species]], and her skin tone is present in both groups, this article does not assume which species she is a part of.
